text: Cold-water immersion improves recovery of muscular power 24 hours after high-intensity exercise compared with passive recovery.
Cold-water immersion improves recovery of muscular power 24 hours after eccentric exercise compared with passive recovery.
Cold-water immersion reduces muscle soreness 24 hours after high-intensity exercise compared with passive recovery.
Cold-water immersion improves perceived feelings of recovery 24 hours after high-intensity exercise compared with passive recovery.
Cold-water immersion reduces serum creatine kinase levels 24 hours after high-intensity exercise, indicating reduced muscle damage.
Cold-water immersion does not significantly improve recovery of strength performance following either eccentric or high-intensity exercise.

Shorter cold-water immersion durations and lower water temperatures produce larger beneficial effects on serum creatine kinase reduction and endurance performance recovery after high-intensity exercise.
